$comment: Shared evidence contract for the /sap-diagnose suite. Every reader (/sap-st22, /sap-sm13, /sap-sm12, /sap-slg1, /sap-sm37, /sap-queues, /sap-sm21) writes ONE file of this shape to {RUN_DIR}\evidence_<source>.json. The correlate helper (sap_diagnose_correlate.ps1) consumes every evidence_*.json in the run dir. All timestamps are SERVER-LOCAL in yyyyMMddHHmmss. All business text passes through log_redact_keys before being written. /sap-st22 in --deep mode additionally opens each in-scope dump and fills the event's include/line fields plus a dump_detail object (see event_shape.dump_detail) — this is what /sap-fix-incident consumes to root-cause a short dump.

properties.source: Reader id — ST22 | SM13 | SM12 | SLG1 | SM37 | QUEUES | SM21 | GW_LOG
properties.status: ok | skipped — 'skipped' means the reader did not run (no auth / not installed / timeout); correlate records it and continues
properties.reason: Free text — populated when status=skipped or when truncated
properties.truncated: true when the reader hit its top-N cap (correlate surfaces this LOUDLY; never silent)
properties.total_count: Total matches found before the cap (>= events.length when truncated)
properties.events: Array of normalized event records (see event shape)

event_shape.id: Stable unique id within the run, e.g. 'SM13-1'. Other events reference it in explicit_links.
event_shape.ts: Server-local timestamp, yyyyMMddHHmmss (14 digits). Empty allowed but disables temporal correlation for this event.
event_shape.severity: A | E | W | I | S  (Abort/Error/Warning/Info/Success — SAP MessageType codes; language-independent)
event_shape.object_keys: Object {KEY:VALUE} of business keys, e.g. { "VBELN": "0000012345" }. Drives business-key correlation across sources.
event_shape.drilldown: How the operator views this in the GUI, e.g. 'ST22 → 20260603/143207'
event_shape.explicit_links: Array of strings. Each may be another event's id, a business key value, a 'KEY:VALUE' token, or any value present in another event's tech{}. Produces a HIGH-confidence correlation edge.
event_shape.dump_detail: OPTIONAL. Present only on ST22 events read in --deep mode. The deep open of the dump also fills the top-level include/line fields. Shape: { detail_status, exception_class, short_text, failing_include, failing_line, source_extract, call_stack, chosen_variables }. detail_status is the honesty marker: 'ok' (failing line + snippet captured), 'partial' (dump opened but body not scrapeable — typically an HTML-rendered dump; exception/program still known from the list level), or 'skipped' (could not open; reason in source_extract is empty). source_extract = [{ "line": "123", "text": "    lv = a / b.", "is_error": true }] (the error line is anchored on the locale-independent '>>>>>' marker). call_stack = [{ "include": "...", "line": "...", "event": "..." }] (best-effort). chosen_variables = [{ "name": "...", "value": "..." }] (best-effort, may be empty). Consumers MUST treat 'partial'/'skipped' as 'not certified' — never as 'no defect here'.
